Hey, what's going everyone? Just a quick overview. You'll see multiple scripts inside of the lead nurture process. If you are doing free for your advertising, then use the traditional script. If you were doing non-free, then I'd recommend you actually use an HTTP script, which is a high-ticket program script, for these set calls, and you can either go for the credit card and build them for the for the appointment, or you can have the credit card on file as a collateral for them to show up to make sure that if they don't show you would build them, kind of like a cancellation type fee. They are different setups. There's different positioning. If your advertising is non-free, you're going to have to increase your price. That being said, the lead quality is going to go up. The lead volume is going to go down, and so you'll probably be looking at three-ish times, four times, the lead cost, but at the same and the same angle, you'll close a higher percentage, and you can easily, if you do the HTTP set script, you can easily close $1,200 sales for people who have paid for that appointment for that first, you know, six weeks. So anywho, it is entirely up to you. It depends on which way you're going, whether you're doing free or non-free. Like I said, the lead cost will be about three or four times higher with non-free versus free, but you'll have super high close rates, and your percentage of leads converted will be higher. So it's a little bit of a math question. You can try one, and then the other, it's up to you both ways work. So that being said, if you see multiple scripts, and one is a super short script, that's going to be the free script, which the entire objective there is really to get them scheduled. There's a second script underneath of that that's sort of like the miracle setter script, which is kind of an in-between. There you're really just getting the card and you're not going to charge them unless they know show. And then the third script is the probably the strongest script, which will result in 90% plus closes when they walk in the door and the highest price point. So that's kind of like three levels of like how much selling happens on the phone versus in person, and depending on, you know, what what you're advertising as free versus non-free. So the second two are going to be non-free scripts. The first one is going to be best for a free advertising campaign. Okay, so just give me a heads up and yeah, dig in. It's gonna be fun.
